messages.content:
Socio-economic development will affect the ability of countries to achieve sustainable development goals. Which of the following climate change related factors affects developing countries most
 A. Health pandemics
 B. Resource Conflicts
 C. Greenhouse emissions
 D. Drought induced food insecurity
D. Drought induced food insecurity

Which of the following is not a part of the Sustainable Development Goal target to be achieved by 2030?
 A. Equal access to affordable vocational training.
 B. Free higher education to all.
 C. Universal Access to higher quality education.
 D. Providing free primary and Secondary schooling to all.
B. Free higher education to all.

Which of the following is not a part of the Sustainable Development Goal target to be achieved by 2030?
 A. Free higher education to all.
 B. Equal access to affordable vocational training.
 C. Universal Access to higher quality education.
 D. Providing free primary and Secondary schooling to all.
A. Free higher education to all.

What are the four domains of circles of sustainability, used by the United Nations?
 A. Economic, political, and cultural
 B. Economic, ecological, and social
 C. Economic, ecological, and cultural
 D. Economic, social, and cultural
B. Economic, ecological, and social

There is a dual relationship between sustainable development and climate change. Which of the following sustainable development goal captures this relationship
 A. SDG 17
 B. SDG 1
 C. SDG 13
 D. ALL SDGs
C. SDG 13

Which of the following is most suited to building resilience for climate change impacts among rural communities in Africa
 A. All of the above, except “a”
 B. Knowledge-based education
 C. Skills based education
 D. Community-Based Development education
D. Community-Based Development education

Universities have an important role to play in addressing climate change and other environmental issues. Which of the following do you think universities are presently NOT doing very well
 A. Conducting research on the causes and impacts of climate change
 B. Providing students with the knowledge and skills needed to understand and address the challenges of climate change
 C. Adopting sustainable practices on their own campuses
 D. Developing innovations for climate change mitigations
C. Adopting sustainable practices on their own campuses

Education is important in Climate change education. Which of the following is fundamental to all the other factors listed through the following except
 A. Creating knowledge
 B. Understanding ecosystems
 C. Holding leaders accountable
 D. Raising awareness
 E. Finding solutions
C. Holding leaders accountable

According to the Asian Development Bank, if 16% students in high/middle-income countries were to receive climate change education, we could see a nearly reduction of carbon dioxide by 2050
 A. 11 gigaton
 B. 99 gigaton
 C. 19 gigaton
 D. 1 gigaton
A. 11 gigaton

To attract the interest of the youth in climate change issues, which of the following would you consider to be the most effective
 A. Strengthen university climate change education
 B. Expand investments at the intersection of sustainability and digitalization
 C. Build green skills in the workforce
 D. Strengthen inter-disciplinary climate studies
B. Expand investments at the intersection of sustainability and digitalization

The status of education policy for climate change can be considered as
 A. Good
 B. Very good
 C. Inadequate
 D. Very inadequate
C. Inadequate
